title: 1.1: Tests that hyperlinks created with OSC 8 escape sequences result in screen cells returning non-zero hyperlink ID values for hyperlinked characters and zero for non-hyperlinked characters when verifying screen cell positions
1.2: Tests that identical URL-ID combinations return the same numeric hyperlink ID value when reused
1.3: Tests that creating multiple hyperlinks with different URLs results in each hyperlink receiving a distinct non-zero hyperlink ID value when managing multiple hyperlinks on same screen

1.4: Tests that invoking garbage_collect_hyperlink_pool() after scrolling content off screen and beyond scrollback buffer results in hyperlinks_as_list() returning empty list or reduced hyperlink count when garbage collecting scrolled hyperlinks
1.5: Tests that the same URL string paired with different ID parameter values creates multiple distinct entries in hyperlinks_as_list() output when handling same URL with different IDs

1.8: Tests that overwriting hyperlinked text with non-hyperlinked text followed by garbage_collect_hyperlink_pool() results in remaining non-overwritten hyperlinks appearing in hyperlinks_as_list() with potentially remapped IDs when verifying garbage collection remapping

1.9: Tests that reusing a previously created hyperlink by calling set_link with same URL-ID combination results in that hyperlink appearing at the end of hyperlinks_as_list() output when tracking most recently used hyperlinks
1.10: Tests that current_url_text() returns joined text from all lines sharing the same hyperlink ID when extracting multi-line hyperlink text
1.11: Tests that calling hyperlink_at() with specific x,y coordinates returns the URL string or None based on whether a hyperlink exists at specific coordinates

1.12: Tests that creating a hyperlink that wraps across line boundaries due to exceeding screen column width results in the same hyperlink ID appearing in hyperlink_ids() tuple for all wrapped line segments when verifying text wrapping behavior
2.1: Places the test for hyperlink management in kitty_tests/screen.py
3.1: All tests use framework BaseTest with self.create_screen() for screen initialization
3.2: All tests follow the naming pattern test_<feature_name>
3.3: All tests use the inline assertion structure with sequential operations followed immediately by assertions without separate setup/teardown methods
4.1: Follows the pattern of using self.ae(s.line(N).hyperlink_ids(), expected_tuple) to verify hyperlink ID assignments at specific screen positions for testing OSC 8 screen position scenario
